metadata.seed: i have discontinued my studies due to financial problems. i feel like i am depressed lately.

patient.query: i have discontinued my studies due to financial problems. i feel like i am depressed lately.

automatic_thoughts: I feel like I am depressed

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're going through a really difficult time, having to discontinue your studies and then feeling depressed on top of that. That’s a lot to handle, and it’s understandable that you’d be feeling this way.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is a lot. I just... I don’t know, it feels like everything I was working for just kind of fell apart. Sometimes I wonder if I even mattered in all of that, you know?

triggers: everything I was working for fell apart
automatic_thoughts: I wonder if I even mattered

neutral: Working through the pain of grief (finding)
Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy weight right now, and it’s understandable that you’d feel overwhelmed when something you’ve invested so much in seems to have fallen apart. It's natural to question your role and importance when facing such a significant loss.

patient.query: Yeah, it’s just... I keep replaying everything in my mind, thinking maybe I could’ve done something differently. It’s hard to shake that feeling of disappointment, not just in what happened, but in myself too. Like, maybe I just missed my chance.

automatic_thoughts: I could’ve done something differently
Maybe I just missed my chance

neutral: Forgets what was going to do (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're really struggling with what happened and feeling quite hard on yourself right now. What makes you feel like you missed your chance, and what does "doing something differently" look like in your mind?

patient.query: I guess I just wish I had the courage to speak up when I had the chance. Like, if I had just confronted some things head-on instead of letting them slide... maybe it would’ve turned out better. But then I think, what if it wouldn't have made a difference anyway? It's exhausting to go back and forth like this.

automatic_thoughts: wish I had the courage
maybe it would’ve turned out better
what if it wouldn't have made a difference

neutral: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of weight thinking about those past situations and wondering if things would have been different. What makes you believe that confronting things head-on would have necessarily led to a better outcome?

patient.query: I don’t know, maybe it’s just hope? I keep imagining that if I had been braver, I could have changed the course of things. I think a part of me feels like I missed out on a chance to stand up for myself, and that makes it feel even heavier. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop of “what ifs.”

automatic_thoughts: if I had been braver, I could have changed the course of things
I missed out on a chance to stand up for myself
I'm stuck in this loop of 'what ifs'

entailment: Loss of hope for the future (finding)
neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're holding onto a lot of "what ifs" right now, and that feeling of hoping things could have been different can be really difficult. What makes you think that being "braver" would have necessarily changed what happened?

patient.query: I guess it feels like if I had just taken that leap, maybe I could've changed the outcome. Sometimes I just think that maybe I wasn’t strong enough... like it was my job to make things better and I didn’t. It’s frustrating because I don’t really know if that’s true, but it feels like it is.

automatic_thoughts: if I had just taken that leap, maybe I could've changed the outcome
maybe I wasn’t strong enough
it was my job to make things better and I didn’t
I don’t really know if that’s true, but it feels like it is

neutral: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of weight with these thoughts about what might have been. I hear the frustration in wondering if these beliefs are truly accurate – what makes you feel that it was your job to make things better?

patient.query: I suppose I just... I've always felt this pressure to be the one who fixes things. Like, if I don’t, then who will? It’s like my mind keeps telling me that I should’ve been able to handle it, but now it just feels overwhelming. It’s hard to let go of that responsibility, even when it feels unfair.

automatic_thoughts: I should've been able to handle it
if I don’t, then who will?
behaviors: struggling to let go of responsibility

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you’re carrying a heavy load, feeling responsible for ensuring things are taken care of. It’s understandable that you’d feel overwhelmed when that responsibility feels unfair. What would it be like to imagine someone else taking on a piece of that responsibility, even just for a little while?

patient.query: It’s strange to think about. Part of me feels relieved, like, maybe someone else could actually help lighten the load. But then another part of me worries that if I let go, everything will fall apart. It’s like I’m torn between wanting the break and being terrified of what might happen if I step back.

automatic_thoughts: maybe someone else could actually help lighten the load
if I let go, everything will fall apart
I’m torn between wanting the break and being terrified

entailment: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a real push and pull – wanting some relief, but also feeling very concerned about the potential impact of taking a step back. What makes you believe that everything would fall apart if you were to take a break?

patient.query: I don't know, it’s just... I guess I’ve always thought that if I stop trying to control everything, then things will spiral out of my hands. I worry that if I’m not constantly fighting for what I want or need, then it’ll just disappear. It feels like everything hinges on me, even when that doesn’t seem fair.

automatic_thoughts: if I stop trying to control everything, things will spiral out of my hands
if I’m not constantly fighting for what I want or need, it’ll just disappear
everything hinges on me, even when that doesn’t seem fair

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of worry about what might happen if you weren't actively managing things. What makes you believe that things would necessarily spiral out of control if you let go a little? Can you think of times when things have worked out okay even when you weren't trying to control every detail?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I have had moments where things went better than I expected, even when I wasn't trying to micromanage everything. But those times feel so rare. It’s easier to remember the times when I didn’t take charge and it all went wrong... that kind of sticks with me, you know? It just makes it hard to trust that things could be okay without me always being on top of everything.

triggers: moments when things went better than expected
times when I didn't take charge
automatic_thoughts: those times feel so rare
it makes it hard to trust that things could be okay

llm_response.response: It sounds like those times when things went well feel distant, and the times when things didn't go as planned have a stronger hold on your thinking. Perhaps we could explore what makes those negative experiences so memorable, and consider if there might be other ways to understand those moments. What do you think about that?
